title: cerastium pumilum subsp. litigiosum
intro: Cerastium pumilum subsp. litigiosum, also known as the Alpine Mouse-Ear or Snow-in-Summer, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Cerastium pumilum subsp. litigiosum include its ability to soothe irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a popular herbal remedy for various skin issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
